Now let's dig right in here very deeply into the actual eight core tonalities and I'll demonstrate them myself here on camera so you can see how I say them and you'll see the difference when I kind of amock you know what it's like when someone doesn't use the right tonality and then I'll give you the right tonality and you'll see the difference and now it not just moves you logically but most importantly moves you emotionally as well. Okay so number one the first tonality that you must master is something called the tonality of scarcity. S-C-A-R-C-I-T-Y scarcity. It's human nature. Okay one of the fundamental influences of human beings is we want to have possession of things that are in short supply. It's called scarcity and we use it all the time to help us close sales as salespeople. We use it in terms of informational scarcity, the information itself is scarce, the actual product itself is not that many left. It's scarce. We jump on and scarcity combined with urgency, meaning if you don't do it now it'll be gone, causes people to take dramatic, effective and immediate action. Now just so you know as human beings we're hardwired into our DNA to want things that are in short supply. It goes back to the cave mandays when there simply wasn't enough food or shelter around so when things were in short supply we ran for them. It's a protective mechanism. So how do you muster that tonality of scarcity and along with scarcity is something called urgency. Not just that you need to buy something but buy it right now. And the way you do it is through some modification of a whisper. So now watch how I say something a certain way and tell me the impact. We'll just think about the impact here and then I'll say it the right way and you'll see how much more profound the words are. So I say to you, listen I have 16 of these items left and then they're going to be completely gone forever. That's a neutral tonality. I say there's 16 of these items left and then they'll be gone forever. Or I say, listen there's only 16 of these items left and then they're gone forever. Which one sounds better to you? Obviously the second. It has so much more of an impact on someone's psychology than simply saying that something is in short supply. Watch I say, listen it's in really short supply or listen it's in really short supply. There's this tone, it's a whisper with a bit of urgency. I'm going to say it again. Yeah, this is really in short supply which is what you ever go to a place, oh yeah, yeah there's really not that many of these left. And you're like, aha, or the guy says, listen there's really not that many of these left. And you're like what, real? And you're immediately moved and said, uh oh, I bet he's getting you hot, get a little shot of adrenaline. That's how you master this art of scarcity. You just keep saying it yourself into a mirror. You say, listen, you know really there's not that many of these left or listen there's really not that many of these left. And look at yourself and you're just tested again and again, get a partner if you can. Just go back and forth and drill, drill, drill until you master that very simple shifting your voice from yeah, there really aren't that many of these left to listen, there really are not that many of these left. And watch that when you do say it that right way, your results, they go up like 10-fold or even 100-fold, very, very dramatic change. Or saying all I'm asking is give me one shot. That give me one shot urgency, not give me one shot. Just give me one shot. The sincerity in there too, but it's urgency. You got to act now. It's that whisper with a little bit of force. It's not like you have to act now. That's a secret. It's not urgency and it's not scarcity. Scarcity is a lower voice with some kind of zets behind it. So it's listen, the key to making money, that's urgency, the key to making money, the way to get involved. It's a lower voice with some force. Now again, when you use a tonality like that, okay, it is so powerful if you have someone that really wants to make the decision, but they're not so certain of the time frame. They think they have all the time in the world. And all of a sudden you throw in that tonality of urgency and scarcity and you explain to them, listen, you actually don't have the key to making money. Is there either we're running out of supply or there's going to be an announcement of some sort of prices is going up and you say, well, prices are going up or Victoria prices are going up. Which one's more powerful? The second, right? Say in a whisper. Okay. So, that's it. Thank you. Bye. Bye. Bye.
